item.title: Dylan at Newport
item.description: Date: July 25, 1965 Location: Freebody Park, Newport, Rhode Island (Newport Folk Festival) Key figures: Bob Dylan, Mike Bloomfield, Al Kooper, Pete Seeger, Alan Lomax, Peter Yarrow, Albert Grossman On a Sunday evening in Newport, Bob Dylan walked onstage with a Fender Stratocaster and a five-piece e...

item.title: British beat boom
item.description: Rock → British beat boom Location: United Kingdom (Liverpool, then London, then the provinces) Period: 1963–1966 Key genres: Beat music, Merseybeat, British rhythm & blues, mod RYM For about three years in the early sixties, the sound of the British singles chart was three guitars, a hammered ba...

item.title: Greenwich Village folk scene
item.description: Folk → Greenwich Village folk scene Location: Greenwich Village, Manhattan, New York City Period: 1958–1965 Key genres: Folk, topical song, folk rock RYM On any given night in 1962, you could walk down MacDougal Street and hear Appalachian murder ballads in one basement, Delta blues in another, and ...

item.title: Merseybeat
item.description: Pop | Rock → Pop rock → Beat → Merseybeat Location: Liverpool, England Period: 1958–1966 Key genres: Beat music, rock & roll, pop rock RYM Merseybeat was the sound of a port that heard America first: rock and roll and rhythm and blues carried off the Atlantic boats, sped up and sung back in a Sc...

item.title: Mod
item.description: Rock → Mod Location: London, England (primarily Soho and the West End) Period: 1958–1967 Key genres: Rhythm & blues, soul, beat music, freakbeat Playlist: MOD RYM Mod was a youth subculture before it was a musical movement, but the music was always central.

item.title: Delta blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Delta blues Period: c. 1920s–1940s (recorded era); roots c.

item.title: Piedmont blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Piedmont blues Period: c.

item.title: Fife and drum blues
item.description: Blues → Fife and drum blues Period: 1942–present (first recordings); tradition c.

item.title: Brooks — Liner Notes for the Revolution
